title: [ExportVerilog] explicitBitcastAddMul all add/mul
body:
Change the --lowering-options=explicitBitcastAddMul option to not be
gated on a subexpression being in an assign.  This is needed (according
to Spyglass...) anytime an addition or multiplication shows up.

Signed-off-by: Schuyler Eldridge <schuyler.eldridge@sifive.com>

This is a less clever approach than https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/2825.  This simply extends @prithayan's work in https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/2756, but uses this _anytime an addition or multiplication shows up_.  Spyglass is overzealous and seems to think that `(4-bit * 4-bit)` is 8-bit even though the Verilog spec seems to indicate that the the use of parentheses makes this self-determined and the width is the max of the two operands.

This is a janky circumvention of all this by always using explicit width casts.  Some users might find this output more readable as there is zero ambiguity about self-determined vs. context-determined widths.

This fixes lingering lint errors for SiFive designs as long as the `--lowering-options=explicitBitcastAddMul` is used.
